article_title: MethaneSAT's Posthumous Data Dump Reveals Oil and Gas Methane Emissions 50 Percent Higher Than Official Estimates, Triggering Senate Investigation

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: MethaneSAT's Posthumous Data Dump Reveals Oil and Gas Methane Emissions 50 Percent Higher Than Official Estimates, Triggering Senate Investigation
content_preview.summary: Data from the now-silent MethaneSAT satellite shows global oil and gas methane emissions far exceed inventories, prompting a U.S. Senate probe into Permian Basin operators.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Environmental Defense Fund's MethaneSAT satellite fell silent in mid-2025 after barely a year in orbit, but the data it transmitted before losing contact is now reshaping the global picture of fossil fuel methane pollution. A first-of-its-kind global assessment released in February 2026 found that methane emissions from oil and gas operations across 45 producing regions are roughly 50 percent higher than what governments and industry report in official inventories, according to ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Outstanding climate/energy article combining satellite data, policy response, and industry context. Excellent use of specific data points and regulatory contrasts.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources checked. Inside Climate News (global assessment): VERIFIED — confirms 50% higher emissions, 45 regions, Permian Basin 410 mt/hr (4x EPA), Delaware Basin NM 1.2% vs TX 3.1%, Steven Hamburg quote, Oil and Gas Decarbonization Charter 0.2% target, Widyan Basin >20%, Appalachian 0.6%. Inside Climate News (Senate investigation): VERIFIED — confirms Sen. Whitehouse March 19 2026, all 8 named operators, 'significant previously unreported emissions' quote, April 1 deadline. SpaceNews: HTTP 429 (rate limited) but source snapshot captured — confirms GHGSat constellation expansion article. Human-requested article reviewed with heightened scrutiny.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified: MethaneSAT data period (May 2024-June 2025), emission figures, regulatory contrasts, Senate investigation details, operator names, Hamburg quote. No inaccuracies found.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and data-driven despite politically charged subject matter. Presents both emissions data and industry context without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior MethaneSAT or methane satellite monitoring coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent human-requested climate article. All sources verified, claims accurate, strong data journalism. Approved.
